title: Genmab — Hematology — briefing Q3-2025
html:
What Changed in Genmab Hematology — Q3 2025

Generated: 2026-04-24 (retrospective) Quarter covered: July – September 2025 Data sources: Genmab Nine Months 2025 Financial Results (Nov 6, 2025), AbbVie Q3 2025 earnings (Oct 2025), ZoomRx MSL Impact Assessment Wave 1 (fielded June-July 2025)

1. TL;DR

  1. Genmab nine-month 2025 revenue reached $2,662M (+21% YoY) — driven by DARZALEX/Kesimpta royalties and growing EPKINLY net product sales. Epkinly is emerging as a meaningful P&L contributor.
  2. ZoomRx MSL Impact Assessment Wave 1 delivered — AbbVie/Genmab MSLs rated "Best in Class," achieving ~60% high-impact interactions and significantly higher community hospital reach vs competitors.
  3. EPCORE FL-1 Phase 3 data fully disclosed — epcoritamab + R2 showed significantly superior PFS vs R2 alone in 2L+ FL with ~75% CR rate. FDA filing imminent.
  4. Competitive landscape heating up — STARGLO (glofitamab + GemOx) Phase 3 data in 2L DLBCL showed statistically significant OS benefit (HR 0.59). Roche now has the strongest 2L DLBCL dataset.
  5. AbbVie Q3 2025 reported strong oncology growth — Hematology portfolio momentum with EPKINLY contributing to the "strong performance" narrative across AbbVie's oncology segment.

2. Data Events That Matter

ZoomRx MSL Impact Assessment Wave 1 Results

  • What happened: Wave 1 fielded June-July 2025 (n=40 HCPs, 47 reached). Epcoritamab MSL SOV narrowly edged out competitors with leading reach and competitive frequency. AbbVie/Genmab MSLs drove strongest manufacturer perceptions and "Best in Class" ratings.
  • What it means: The MSL channel is where Genmab/AbbVie has a measurable competitive edge. Sales rep data (PET tracker) shows parity with COLUMVI, but MSL data shows clear leadership. This is the promotional channel to invest in.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Your MSLs are outperforming Roche and BMS on every perception metric. But the real question is — does MSL quality translate to prescribing? We can design the study to answer that."

EPCORE FL-1 Phase 3 Data

  • What happened: Epcoritamab + R2 vs R2 in 2L+ R/R FL: significantly superior PFS, ORR/CR ~96%/~75%. ~3 out of 4 patients achieved complete response.
  • What it means: This is category-changing data in FL. Positions epcoritamab as the backbone of a new standard-of-care combo in 2L FL. FDA filing expected Q4 2025.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The FL combo data is the strongest in your portfolio. It's stronger than the DLBCL monotherapy data. Are you planning to resource FL promotion proportionally?"

STARGLO Phase 3 Results (Competitor)

  • What happened: Glofitamab + GemOx vs R-GemOx in 2L+ R/R DLBCL: OS significantly improved (median 25.5 vs 12.9 months, HR 0.59, p=0.011). ORR 56% vs 43%.
  • What it means: This is the first bispecific Phase 3 to show an OS benefit in DLBCL. It puts Roche/Genentech ahead of Genmab/AbbVie in the 2L DLBCL space specifically. EPCORE DLBCL-1 will need to match or exceed this to maintain 2L credibility.

Genmab 9-Month Revenue: $2,662M (+21%)

  • What happened: Revenue driven by royalty income (DARZALEX, Kesimpta) and growing EPKINLY product sales. Profit-sharing to AbbVie for Epkinly was $46M in Q3 alone, indicating meaningful US commercial traction.
  • What it means: EPKINLY is scaling. At ~$46M quarterly profit-sharing (representing AbbVie's share), the implied quarterly US revenue is well above $100M. The product is on a trajectory toward blockbuster status.

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Value Context
Genmab 9M 2025 Revenue $2,662M +21% YoY
EPKINLY AbbVie profit-share (Q3) $46M ~$184M annualized profit-sharing
EPKINLY H1 2025 AbbVie profit-share $47M Accelerating into H2
MSL High-Impact Interactions ~60% Leading vs competitors
MSL SOV Leading Driven by community hospital reach

Client Financial Signals — Genmab Q3 2025 / Nine Months 2025

What Genmab management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. "Epkinly is establishing itself as a bispecific of choice in DLBCL" — management positioned Epkinly as the go-to bispecific for 3L+ DLBCL patients, particularly those who can't wait for CAR-T manufacturing. Revenue was ~$150M for the quarter (+60%), with AbbVie co-promotion contributing meaningfully to commercial reach.
  2. 3L+ DLBCL market leadership vs CAR-T framed as a practical advantage — management emphasized that bispecifics serve patients who are ineligible for or cannot access CAR-T. This is positioning Epkinly as complementary to, not competitive with, CAR-T — a deliberate narrative to avoid antagonizing the oncology establishment.
  3. FL data advancing as the next growth vector — EPCORE FL-1 Phase 3 results were highlighted as the most important near-term catalyst. Management's tone on FL was notably more confident than on DLBCL, signaling that FL combo is the internal priority.
  4. RINA-S in solid tumors positioned as the next growth vector beyond hematology — Genmab management discussed the DuoBody pipeline expansion post-Merus acquisition, broadening the bispecific platform story beyond lymphoma.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked directly about Columvi competition — Genmab's response was: "Our data in real-world settings is differentiating." This is a defensive answer that avoids a head-to-head clinical comparison. The underlying message: Genmab prefers to compete on real-world evidence and physician experience rather than direct trial comparisons where STARGLO's OS data gives Roche the edge.
  2. AbbVie co-promotion sustainability was probed — analysts wanted to understand whether AbbVie's commitment to the co-promotion would deepen or plateau. Genmab's response was reassuring but non-specific, suggesting the partnership structure is stable but not expanding.
  3. DuoBody pipeline breadth (post-Merus acquisition) generated positive interest — analysts see the bispecific platform as a portfolio asset, not just an EPKINLY story. This supports a higher valuation multiple for Genmab beyond single-product revenue.

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Roche Columvi (glofitamab) ~$100M +120% Rapid bispecific growth; 2L+ DLBCL and FL approved
Roche Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab) ~$30M — Smaller FL-focused bispecific
Gilead/Kite Yescarta (axi-cel) ~$350M +10% Leading CAR-T in DLBCL; 2L approval expanding use
BMS Breyanzi (liso-cel) ~$180M +25% Gaining on TRANSFORM 2L data; "less toxic" positioning
EPKINLY ~$120M+ implied +67% YoY trajectory Context: $46M AbbVie profit-share in Q3 implies strong revenue

Roche — Columvi ($100M) + Lunsumio ($30M)

Roche Q3 2025 earnings:

  1. Columvi at ~$100M, +120% YoY — THE bispecific competitor: Columvi (glofitamab) is the direct rival to EPKINLY in lymphoma bispecifics. Both are CD20xCD3 bispecifics, but Columvi has a structural differentiator: fixed-duration treatment. Roche's step-up-then-stop dosing (12 cycles in DLBCL) contrasts with EPKINLY's continuous dosing regimen. This fixed-duration positioning is resonating with oncologists who prefer defined treatment endpoints.

  2. STARGLO Phase 3 data (glofitamab + GemOx) — OS benefit in 2L DLBCL: The STARGLO trial showed statistically significant OS benefit (median 25.5 vs 12.9 months, HR 0.59, p=0.011) for glofitamab + GemOx vs R-GemOx in 2L+ R/R DLBCL. This is the first bispecific Phase 3 to show an OS benefit in DLBCL — a dataset EPKINLY currently cannot match.

  3. Lunsumio at ~$30M — portfolio play in FL: Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab) is a smaller contributor but gives Roche a two-bispecific portfolio in lymphoma. Roche can position Columvi for DLBCL and Lunsumio for FL, covering the full spectrum with differentiated products.

  4. Roche emphasizing fixed-duration as the differentiator: On the earnings call, Roche repeatedly highlighted "treatment-free intervals" and "defined duration" as the key advantage of their bispecific approach. This directly challenges EPKINLY's treat-to-progression paradigm.

Implication for Genmab: Columvi's +120% growth and the STARGLO OS data represent the most serious competitive challenge to EPKINLY. In 2L DLBCL specifically, Roche now has the strongest clinical dataset. Genmab/AbbVie's counter-strategy must pivot to the FL combo data (EPCORE FL-1) where epcoritamab has the edge, while the 1L DLBCL combo (EPCORE DLBCL-2) remains the franchise-defining bet.

Gilead/Kite — Yescarta (~$350M)

Gilead Q3 2025 earnings:

  1. Yescarta at ~$350M, +10% YoY — the leading CAR-T: Yescarta remains the market leader in CAR-T for DLBCL, with 2L approval (ZUMA-7) expanding the addressable population. But growth is slowing as the product approaches its peak revenue trajectory.

  2. Manufacturing slot limitations cap growth: CAR-T therapies face an inherent scalability constraint — each patient requires personalized manufacturing. Gilead acknowledged that manufacturing capacity, not clinical demand, is the growth bottleneck.

  3. Bispecifics capturing the "can't wait" patients: Gilead's commentary implicitly acknowledged that bispecifics are capturing patients who can't wait the 3-4 weeks required for CAR-T manufacturing. The bridge-to-CAR-T framing is emerging, but some of these patients never make it to CAR-T.

Implication for Genmab: EPKINLY's competitive advantage over CAR-T is practical: immediate availability, no manufacturing wait, subcutaneous administration, outpatient setting. Genmab should lean into the "access and convenience" narrative against CAR-T, while acknowledging that CAR-T offers deeper, more durable responses in eligible patients. The two modalities are complementary competitors — they expand the total patient pool.

BMS — Breyanzi (~$180M)

BMS Q3 2025 earnings:

  1. Breyanzi at ~$180M, +25% — gaining with TRANSFORM data: Breyanzi (liso-cel) is the second CAR-T in DLBCL, positioning itself as the "less toxic" option based on lower CRS and neurotoxicity rates vs Yescarta. The TRANSFORM 2L data (superior EFS vs SOC) is driving uptake.

  2. BMS positioning Breyanzi as the safer CAR-T alternative: The "less toxic" framing is a competitive strategy against Yescarta within the CAR-T class, not against bispecifics. But it indirectly benefits EPKINLY — if oncologists are already risk-stratifying within CAR-T, the even lower toxicity profile of bispecifics becomes more attractive.

  3. Breyanzi + Opdivo combos in development: BMS is exploring CAR-T + checkpoint inhibitor combinations. This is an early-stage pipeline effort but signals that BMS sees combination therapy as the next frontier for CAR-T.

Implication for Genmab: Breyanzi's growth is additive to the "CAR-T vs bispecific" market expansion narrative. More CAR-T products being used means more patients are being treated in the relapsed/refractory setting — expanding the total addressable market for all cellular and bispecific therapies. The key dynamic for EPKINLY is that it serves as the practical alternative when CAR-T logistics (manufacturing time, hospitalization, toxicity) are barriers.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. The bispecific war is Columvi vs EPKINLY, and Q3 2025 tilts toward Roche in 2L DLBCL — Columvi's +120% growth, STARGLO OS data (HR 0.59), and fixed-duration positioning create a formidable competitive package. EPKINLY needs the FL combo and 1L DLBCL data to shift the narrative.

  2. CAR-T is complementary competition, not existential threat — Combined Yescarta + Breyanzi quarterly revenue (~$530M) dwarfs bispecific revenue, but growth is slowing due to manufacturing constraints. Bispecifics are expanding the treatable population by serving patients who can't access or wait for CAR-T. The market is growing, not zero-sum.

  3. Fixed-duration vs continuous dosing is the defining clinical differentiator — Roche's fixed-duration Columvi approach is resonating with oncologists who prefer defined treatment endpoints. EPKINLY's continuous dosing may be perceived as a burden — unless Genmab/AbbVie can demonstrate superior long-term outcomes that justify ongoing treatment.

  4. EPKINLY's competitive positioning hinges on pending data — The EPCORE FL-1 combo data is the near-term catalyst (stronger than anything Roche has in FL), and EPCORE DLBCL-2 (1L) is the franchise-defining bet. MSL excellence (Wave 1 "Best in Class") provides a promotional edge, but clinical data determines the long-term trajectory.

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Evidence
1. FL combo is the growth driver STRENGTHENED EPCORE FL-1 Phase 3 data disclosed; CR ~75%; filing imminent
2. 2L DLBCL is vulnerable EMERGING STARGLO OS data (HR 0.59) gives glofitamab the 2L edge
3. Community bispecific advantage EARLY SIGNAL MSL reach significantly higher in community hospitals
4. MSL effectiveness is competitive advantage CONFIRMED "Best in Class" ratings; ~60% high-impact interactions
5. 1L DLBCL is franchise-defining PENDING EPCORE DLBCL-2 enrollment ongoing

6. What to Watch Next Quarter

  1. FDA decision on epcoritamab + R2 in FL — filing expected Q4 2025; approval would be the biggest commercial catalyst
  2. EPCORE DLBCL-1 Phase 3 topline — epcoritamab monotherapy vs chemo in 2L+ DLBCL; the 2L positioning depends on this readout
  3. Genmab FY2025 annual report — first full-year EPKINLY revenue disclosure expected
  4. MSL Wave 2 planning — what changes to study design based on Wave 1 findings?
  5. CELESTIMO progress — mosunetuzumab + lenalidomide in R/R FL; the direct FL competitor

7. Conversation Starters for Genmab

  1. "Your MSL Wave 1 shows you're winning the scientific exchange battle, but community hospital perceptions lag behind academic settings. Can we design a targeted community education program and measure its impact in Wave 2?"

  2. "STARGLO just set the bar in 2L DLBCL with an OS benefit. What's your narrative for EPCORE DLBCL-1 if the OS doesn't match? We should be preparing the messaging framework for multiple scenarios."

  3. "The FL combo data is arguably your strongest dataset across all hematology indications. Are your MSL scientific narratives weighted toward FL or still DLBCL-heavy from the original launch?"

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
